Q & A
programme.sub-title: Air & Space Museum's Teasel Muir-Harmony on the History of the U.S. Space Program
programme.desc: The Smithsonian Air and Space Museum's Teasel Muir-Harmony discusses the history of the U.S. space program, from the creation of NASA in 1958 to Neil Armstrong walking on the moon in 1969, and the missions that followed.

programme.title: Reel America
programme.desc: On April 11, 1970, Apollo 13 blasted off on what was to be the third NASA mission to land humans on the moon, but a crisis nearly left the three astronauts stranded in space.

programme.title: Career of Former NASA Flight Director Gerald Griffin
programme.desc: American History TV interviews former NASA flight director Gerald Griffin on his career in the American space program from the Gemini program to the Shuttle missions. He worked with NASA from 1964-1986.

programme.title: C-SPAN Tours the National Air & Space Museum
programme.desc: C-SPAN tours the Smithsonian's National Air & Space Museum in Washington, D.C., where we look at Apollo 11's lunar lander, Charles Lindbergh's plane that flew across the Atlantic, the X-15 that Neil Armstrong flew, and much more.

programme.title: Reel America
programme.desc: This NASA film showcases the Skylab space station mission that ran from 1973 to 1974. Astronaut crews conducted scientific experiments on board Skylab to test whether humans could live and work in space.

programme.title: Reel America
programme.sub-title: Reel America Voyager II's Rendezvous With Saturn 1986
programme.desc: A U.S. Information Agency video from 1986 shows discoveries photographed by Voyager II as the satellite makes its closest approach to Saturn in August 1981.

programme.title: Reel America
programme.desc: 1979 NASA film about the history of efforts to explore Mars including the 1964-1971 Mariner fly-bys and the Viking landings in 1976.

programme.title: History of the Space Shuttle Program
programme.desc: C-SPAN's American History TV visits the Air & Space Museum's Udvar-Hazy Center to get an up-close look at the Space Shuttle Discovery and explore the history of the shuttle program.

programme.title: Reel America
programme.sub-title: Space Shuttle -- Mission to the Future -- 1981
programme.desc: A NASA film explains the space shuttle program using a simulated flight of Columbia and emphasizes international cooperation in the program.

programme.title: Reel America

programme.title: Reel America

programme.desc: A NASA documentary covers the April 12, 1981, launch of the first space shuttle, Columbia.

programme.desc: Commander Steven Swanson talks by video from the International Space Station.
